subject: Fwd: Privileged and Confidential Legal Advice
body:

Sincerely,

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison  Avenue
Fourth Floor
New York, New York 10022
Telephone:  212-750-1176
Telecopier: 212-750-0381

e-mail:  dkiesq@aol.com

**************Start the year off right.  Easy ways to stay in shape.     
http://body.aol.com/fitness/winter-exercise?NCID=aolcmp00300000002489


From: "John Amerling" <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
Subject: Privileged and Confidential Legal Advice
Date: Wed, 23 Jan 2008 19:48:14 GMT
To: "'Darren Indyke'" <dkiesq@aol.com>

Today I was advised of a problem with the dock filing locally. As you may
recall we just  filed and joint application for after the fact approval of
the dock, the dock intake line and the bulkhead with the ACOE. They are
happy with the filing. I then went to file the counterpart with the locals
and was told there might be a problem with the fact that the intake line has
been in operation before the permit was issued as they already know. DPNR
wanted to come see the line and did so today. I met them at the island.  

 

The following is the background and the current status: 

1.       Prior to my watch the consultants filed for permission for approval
of a temporary intake line which, apparently, had been put into service off
the dock by the island without a request for a permit some years ago; it
operated for a long time and was the subject of the EAR which was submitted
in 2005 when McComb and crowd sought to get the ACOE and all dock problems
off the mark

2.       On August 30, 2006, while I was still with RR, DPNR came out to the
island, J.P. Oriole (water) and Algem Peterson (land); you may recall the
JEE was there and participated in the meeting; JEE will recall what I am
relaying below

3.       At that time we went over the line, changes to the barge landing
area and other issues on the water side such as the floating dock and swim
platform: J.P., in JEE's presence, asked that all projects be wrapped into a
request for an amendment to the existing dock permit which had been locally
approved, but which was still the subject of the unresolved ACOE violation;
we were told that that filing should be made after the fact when we finally
go ACOE to agree to hear our application

4.       At the same time Algem said that we should consolidate all of our
CZM permit requests under one file number which she gave me and each would
be treated as a minor modification

5.       I relayed all this both to McComb and Jamie Casner

6.       For the next year we filed and obtained a number of minor
amendments; in the approved drawings for each we showed the intake line to
make sure that we were covered until the ACOE matter was resolved as
suggested by J.P.

7.       When we filed for the RO/Gen permit last September I visited J.P.
asking again how to deal with the line since we could still not then file
for a permit with the ACOE violation still pending, but it appeared that I
was going to be able, finally, to get an audience at ACOE; he again advised
that we should seek an amendment to the dock permit when ACOE was resolved; 

8.       In November we came to terms with ACOE; in early December I took
the forms to DPNR and went over with J.P. how to present our requests now
that the logjam had been broken: he advised that we file a joint application
on Form 935 and request approval of the line by letter to the CZM committee
asking for an amendment to the Dock permit. 

9.       I prepared the forms and called JP, went over the filing again and
asked him to compute the fee; he did; I got a check  from Rich and I went to
file at the agency; I was told that CZM wanted to come out and look at the
line today

10.   Today they indicated that they would have to issue a notice of
violation which we would have to clean up before they processed the filing
which I was making; I claimed foul since JP had not only suggested but
blessed what we were doing; it was, however, pointless to get overly
aggressive since we have to live with these guys

11.   I have investigated the background to find the following:

a.       Lovango Cay is in exactly the same position as we are concerning
and ro line under their dock

b.      A federal audit in December concluded that the local agency should
do more fining than trying to work things out

c.       So the agency is into fining, but there may be ways to do something
in lieu of a fine which is what Paul Hoffman did in the past as I understand
it. 

12.   Fines are not desirable and once a fine is levied the past becomes
prologue for future problems; I suspect that the fine here will be $10,000
or less, but I really do not know

13.   I have called Tom Bolt to see how they are handling this problem; our
case differs in that we want to have this go away quickly so that we finish
this drawn ACOE process

14.   I have not heard back yet from Tom; the notice of violation will be
issued in a day or two and I will be asked to come down to pick it up; their
counsel is reasonable and wants to get this out of the way asap; the
situation is infuriating since we told them what the problem was and
followed their suggested solution; JP was embarrassed if that means anything

15.   This too will pass ultimately, but it will take a little time to iron
out; in the meantime its pendency will not affect our pending land side
permit request for changes to the existing compound; we are fully permitted
for the office and the RO/Gen building

 

I have given you a lot of information here, but I thought it the best way to
set the table for a call at your convenience. 

 

Lafayette Contractors, LLC

6100 Red Hook Quarters, Suite A2-7

St. Thomas, VI 00802

340 779-8054 (O)

340 779-8055 (FAX)

340 690-7194 (Cell)
